title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC
text:
why we report data beyond COVID-19, RSV, and flu. 
December 5, 2025 release 
1. Updates to the National and State Acute Respiratory Illness (ARI) Thresholds for 2025-26 Season
What: 
Refreshed acute respiratory illness (ARI) thresholds to tailor state-specific categories using three years of historical data. 
Why: 
Enhances the precision and relevance of ARI reporting, supporting more informed public health decisions at the state level. 
Uses the same methodology as previous seasons, with the addition of the 2024–25 season to the historical baseline (previously based on 2022–23 and 2023–24 data). 
November 21, 2025 release 
1. Vaccination Trends Publication Page
What: 
The 2025 respiratory illness season is now underway, so data from the National Immunization Survey (NIS), the Vaccination Trends page, and its accompanying visuals will be published on a weekly basis. 
Why: 
Surveillance for the 2024-2025 season ended on April 30, marking the beginning of the off-season. Surveillance for the 2025-2026 season began on October 1. 
2. Addition of RSV R t on Activity Levels Page
What: 
Epidemic trend estimates (R t ) for RSV will be introduced at the state level, using the same methodology currently applied to COVID-19 and influenza. These estimates will be displayed alongside existing metrics to provide a consistent, comparative view across pathogens. 
Why:
